content_en: So (for example) whoever's mind becomes flooded with thoughts of disbelief, but they are only thoughts, without him intending to have them carried out, and he then rids himself of these thoughts right after they occur, he is not a disbeliever nor is there any sin on him."[311 [30] Bukhaaree [31] The proof of this position is from hadith reported by Muslim: Narrated by Abu Hurayrah ...;.,: "Some people from amongst the Companions of the Messenger ~ came to him and said: 'Verily we perceive in our minds that which every one of us considers it too grave to express.' He (the Prophet ~ ) said: 'Do you really perceive it?' They said: 'Yes.' Upon this he remarked: ((;i~~I &? ~\S)) 'That is the faith manifest.' This hadith shows the Companions' zeal and enthusiasm in commanding themselves with good and forbidding themselves from evil, such that they Golden Advice Series 04 The reason for these thoughts being excused is because thoughts are impossible to prevent. Rather, one is only able to prevent those thoughts from becoming settled and established in one's mind. This is why the heart's being resolute and determined on these thoughts is forbidden. So whenever these thoughts, which consist of backbiting or any other sin, present themselves in your mind, it is an obligation on you to repel them, turn away from them and mention some excuses or explanations that will change what seems apparent. Abu Haamid AI-Ghazaalee sill u-_; said in Al-lhyaa: "If bad thoughts should occur in your heart, then these are from the whisperings of the Devil, which he has placed in you. So you must deny and reject them because he is indeed the most wicked of evildoers (Faasiq). And Allaah has stated: t~:: i,, -! ·1 r~/-::-: G r~ ~ 5:, ,:ii t>./1: &Ji (-!ls~ '-" ~ u ~ .} . ,;. ~ . !;. '.>-4 ., '"f: • "O you who believe! If a wicked person (i.e. Faasiq) comes to you with news, then verify it, lest you harm people without realizing it (i.e. out of ignoranc \ and 1 e 1 a u e ome regretful for what you've done [AI-Hujuraat (49) : 6] So it is not permissible for you to believe lblees (the Devil). And if there is some sign that indicates that he is corrupt, yet he implies the opposite of that, then it is not permissible to hold bad would even fight against the evil notions that passed through their minds. But as it is impossible to prevent such thoughts from occurring every J""JW and then, they asked the Prophet ~ about this. His response to them meant that their trying to repel these thoughts showed their strong Eemaan (Faith) and love for good. Guarding the Tongue thoughts (for him). Among the signs that indicate one holding bad thoughts (for someone) is that your heart changes with him from the way it used to be, and that you run away from him and find him to be unbearable. And that you are lazy when it comes to having concern for him, showing kindness to him and being worried when he does evil. And indeed the Devil comes close to one's heart when the slightest trace of defects show in people, and he places this in you, while you think (these thoughts) occur due to your astuteness, intelligence and quick alertness. But the believer sees with the light of Allaah. So this person is in reality speaking with the deceptions and the evil plots of the Devil. And if a trustworthy person informs you of this, then do not believe him nor reject him, in order that you will not have bad thoughts about either of them. Whenever some evil thoughts about another Muslim come into your mind, then let that make you increase in your showing concern and being kind to him because this will enrage the Devil and repel him from you. So he will not place such thoughts in you afterward out of fear that it will only cause you to increase in your supplication for that person. And whenever you come to know of a defect or a mistake in another Muslim based on some proofs, which cannot be denied, then advise him in privacy and do not let the Devil deceive you such that he invites you and leads you towards backbiting him. And when you admonish him, then do not admonish him while you are happy and pleased that you have knowledge of his deficiency. So it is as if he is looking at you with the eyes of awe and respect while you are looking down at him with condescending eyes. Rather, make your intention in that to free him from this sin, Golden Advice Series 04 while you are distressed over him, just as you are distressed when some deficiency enters in you. And his getting rid of that deficiency without you having to admonish him (i.e.
content_id:
Sehingga (misalnya) barang siapa yang benaknya dipenuhi dengan pikiran-pikiran kekufuran, namun itu hanyalah pikiran semata tanpa niat untuk melaksanakannya, kemudian ia segera membuang pikiran-pikiran itu setelah kemunculannya, maka ia bukanlah seorang kafir dan tidak ada dosa atasnya.

Alasan mengapa pikiran-pikiran semacam itu dimaafkan adalah karena pikiran tidak mungkin dicegah kemunculannya. Yang dapat dilakukan hanyalah mencegah pikiran-pikiran itu dari menetap dan mengakar di benak seseorang. Inilah mengapa hati yang bertekad bulat dan mantap pada pikiran-pikiran semacam itu diharamkan. Maka setiap kali pikiran-pikiran yang berupa ghibah atau dosa lainnya muncul di benakmu, wajib atasmu untuk menolaknya, berpaling darinya, dan menyebutkan alasan atau penjelasan yang dapat mengubah apa yang tampak.

Imam Abu Hamid al-Ghazali rahimahullah berkata dalam Al-Ihya': "Jika pikiran-pikiran buruk muncul dalam hatimu, maka itu adalah bisikan-bisikan setan yang ia tanamkan dalam dirimu. Maka engkau harus menyangkal dan menolaknya karena ia adalah orang yang paling fasik. Dan Allah telah berfirman: 'Hai orang-orang yang beriman, jika datang kepadamu orang fasik membawa suatu berita, maka periksalah dengan teliti, agar kamu tidak menimpakan suatu musibah kepada suatu kaum tanpa mengetahui keadaannya yang menyebabkan kamu menyesal atas perbuatanmu itu.' (Al-Hujurat [49]: 6) Maka tidak diperbolehkan bagimu untuk mempercayai Iblis."

Di antara tanda-tanda seseorang menyimpan prasangka buruk (kepada seseorang) adalah bahwa hatimu berubah terhadapnya dari keadaan semula, engkau menghindarinya dan merasa tidak nyaman dengannya, serta engkau malas untuk peduli kepadanya, berbuat baik kepadanya, dan merasa khawatir ketika ia berbuat keburukan.

Sesungguhnya setan mendekati hati seseorang ketika ia melihat sedikit saja kekurangan pada diri orang lain, lalu ia menanamkan hal itu dalam dirimu, sementara engkau mengira pikiran-pikiran itu muncul karena kecerdasanmu, kepintaranmu, dan kewaspadaanmu. Namun seorang mukmin melihat dengan cahaya Allah.

Jika seseorang yang terpercaya memberitahumu tentang hal ini, maka janganlah engkau mempercayainya maupun menolaknya, agar engkau tidak berprasangka buruk terhadap salah satu dari keduanya.

Setiap kali pikiran-pikiran buruk tentang seorang Muslim melintas dalam benakmu, jadikanlah itu sebagai pendorong bagimu untuk semakin bertambah perhatian dan kebaikanmu kepadanya. Karena hal itu akan membuat setan murka dan mengusirnya darimu. Maka ia tidak akan menanamkan pikiran-pikiran semacam itu dalam dirimu setelah itu, karena khawatir hal itu hanya akan membuatmu semakin bertambah dalam berdoa untuk orang tersebut.

Dan setiap kali engkau mengetahui aib atau kesalahan seorang Muslim berdasarkan bukti-bukti yang tidak dapat disangkal, maka nasihati dia secara pribadi dan janganlah biarkan setan menipu dan menggodamu menuju ghibah terhadapnya. Dan ketika engkau menasihatinya, janganlah engkau menasihatinya dalam keadaan senang dan bangga karena mengetahui kekurangannya. Seolah-olah ia memandangmu dengan mata penuh kekaguman dan rasa hormat sementara engkau memandang ke bawah kepadanya dengan mata yang merendahkan. Sebaliknya, jadikanlah niatmu adalah untuk membebaskannya dari dosa tersebut, sementara engkau sedih karenanya, sebagaimana engkau sedih ketika suatu kekurangan menimpa dirimu sendiri.
